title: Neighborhood Draft
description: You organized a neighborhood draft for backyard football. No one else understood the rules.

choices.text: Pick the teams yourself. You are the commissioner.
choices.flavor: Draft order determined by rock-paper-scissors. Appeals process: none.

choices.text: Let everyone pick and just play
choices.flavor: Less drama, more football. The game starts in under five minutes for once.

title: Unofficial Scorekeeper
description: You are keeping score in a game where nobody asked for a scorekeeper.

choices.text: Announce the score loudly after every play
choices.flavor: 'That is 14-7!' Nobody cares. You care enough for everyone.

choices.text: Keep it to yourself and use it as motivation
choices.flavor: You know you are down by one touchdown. Nobody else does. Quiet competitiveness.

title: The Kid to Beat
description: There is one kid who is clearly better than everyone else. You have been studying how they play.

choices.text: Challenge them directly and compete head to head
choices.flavor: You lost. But you made them work for it. They noticed.

choices.text: Practice in secret and wait for the right moment
choices.flavor: Extra hours in the yard. When the moment comes, you will be ready.

title: Cannot Stop
description: Nobody told you to keep going. You just did not want to stop throwing the ball against the garage.

choices.text: Keep going until Mom yells dinner
choices.flavor: Thud. Thud. Thud. The neighbors stopped asking if you were okay. Mom yelled twice.

choices.text: Try to beat your own record from yesterday
choices.flavor: Yesterday was 47 catches in a row. Today is 52. Tomorrow the record falls again.

title: The Weaker Kid
description: A smaller kid wants to join your game. He is not very good. Your team is up next.

choices.text: Let them play. Put them somewhere they can help.
choices.flavor: You put them on the line. They try hard. Your team wins anyway. Good leadership.

choices.text: Tell them next game. You need to win this one.
choices.flavor: You won the game. The kid watched from the sideline. It felt different than you expected.

title: No Fear
description: A big kid is running straight at you during backyard football. Everyone else gets out of the way.

choices.text: Lower your shoulder and meet them head on
choices.flavor: BOOM. You both go down. The other kids go silent. Then someone says 'that kid is crazy.' Badge of honor.

choices.text: Stand your ground and wrap up the tackle
choices.flavor: Clean stop. Textbook. The fearless kid is also becoming a smart player.

title: New House
description: Your family is moving to a different part of town. New neighborhood, new school, new everything.

choices.text: Be excited about the fresh start
choices.flavor: New room, new kids to compete with. Change is fuel.
It is hard leaving your crew. But adapting is a skill you will use forever.
